hero.title: JSON Formatter
hero.tagline: Paste your JSON below, click Format, and get clean, readable output instantly — no sign-up required. Invalid JSON is caught and reported with the line number so you can fix errors quickly.

whatIs.heading: What is JSON
whatIs.body: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight text format used to represent structured data. It is both human-readable and machine-parseable, and is the dominant format for data exchange in REST APIs, configuration files, and inter-process communication.

whenToUse.heading: When to use JSON
whenToUse.body: Use JSON when you need a simple, interoperable way to serialize structured data for transmission or storage — particularly in web contexts where JavaScript is involved on one or both ends.

howFormatterWorks.heading: How the JSON formatter works
howFormatterWorks.body: The formatter parses the raw input as JSON (using the browser's built-in JSON.parse), then serializes it back with JSON.stringify using a configurable indent level. If parsing fails, the error is caught and the approximate line number of the problem is reported.

prettyFormat.heading: JSON in pretty format
prettyFormat.body: Pretty-printed JSON inserts line breaks between key–value pairs and arrays, and indents nested objects and arrays. This makes the structure immediately visible without changing the data.

usefulWhen.heading: Useful when
usefulWhen.item1: Debugging API responses that are returned as minified JSON.
usefulWhen.item2: Reviewing configuration objects or data payloads before deployment.
usefulWhen.item3: Spotting structural errors in JSON before they reach production code.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.q1: Q: Does formatting change the data?
faq.a1: A: No — formatting only changes whitespace; all values and structure remain identical.
faq.q2: Q: Can the formatter fix broken JSON?
faq.a2: A: No — it reports the approximate line of the error but cannot repair malformed input.
faq.a3: A: Two spaces, which is the most widely used convention for JSON.
faq.q4: Q: Is the JSON processed on my device?
faq.a4: A: Yes — all parsing and formatting runs in the browser; no data is sent to a server.
faq.q5: Q: Why does the formatter report an error on a different line than I expect?
faq.a5: A: JSON parsers often continue past an error before failing; the reported line is the best approximation the parser can provide.

howItWorks.step1: Paste or type raw JSON into the input panel.
howItWorks.step2: Click Validate & Format (or press Ctrl+Enter) to run the parser.
howItWorks.step3: Review formatted output and copy or download as needed.
howItWorks.step4: Fix any parse errors indicated by the error panel and reformat.

howToUse.heading: How to use
howToUse.step1: 1: Paste or type your JSON into the input field below.
howToUse.step3: 3: Review the formatted output and click Copy to copy it.

errors.empty: Please enter some JSON to format.

guide.title: How to Read and Fix JSON Faster (A Practical Guide for Developers)
guide.intro: If you've ever worked with APIs, logs, or config files, you've seen messy minified JSON. Technically correct… but painful to read. That's where a JSON formatter becomes essential.
guide.cta: 👉 In this guide, you'll learn how to turn messy JSON into readable structure, debug errors faster, and work more efficiently with API data.

whatIs.heading: 📦 What Is JSON (Quick Refresher)
whatIs.body: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data format used to exchange structured data between systems. It's used in APIs, backend services, config files, and databases. Machines love JSON — humans, not so much (when it's messy).

whatDoes.heading: 🔍 What Does a JSON Formatter Do?
whatDoes.body: A JSON formatter takes raw or minified JSON and adds indentation, organizes nested structures, and makes data readable. It doesn't change the data — only how it looks.

whySlows.heading: 😵 Why Raw JSON Slows You Down
whySlows.item1: 🔍 Hard to scan — nested objects become dense, confusing, and easy to misread.
whySlows.item2: 🐞 Debugging becomes painful — one missing comma or bracket breaks everything and is hard to locate.
whySlows.item3: ⏱ Wasted time — you spend more time reading structure instead of solving problems.

whenToUse.heading: ⚡ When You Should Use a JSON Formatter
whenToUse.item1: 🔌 API Responses — most APIs return minified JSON; formatting reveals structure immediately.
whenToUse.item2: 🐞 Debugging Errors — formatter + validator shows exactly where JSON breaks.
whenToUse.item3: ⚙️ Config Files — cleaner structure means fewer mistakes in .json settings.
whenToUse.item4: 📄 Log Analysis — formatting nested JSON blobs in logs helps spot issues quickly.

bestPractices.heading: 🚀 Best Practices for Working With JSON
bestPractices.item1: ✅ Keep it valid — always use double quotes and match brackets correctly.
bestPractices.item2: ✅ Format before debugging — don't debug raw JSON; always format first.
bestPractices.item3: ✅ Use consistent indentation — 2 spaces is the most common standard.
bestPractices.item4: ✅ Avoid over-nesting — deep nesting is hard to read and hard to maintain.
bestPractices.item5: ✅ Validate early — catch errors before using JSON in code.

proTips.heading: ⚡ Pro Tips
proTips.item2: 🧩 Break large JSON into smaller parts

safety.heading: 🔐 Is It Safe to Use Online JSON Formatters?
safety.body: Most modern tools run directly in your browser and don't send data to servers. Still, avoid pasting API keys or sensitive production data.

faq.heading: ❓ FAQ
faq.q1: Does formatting change my JSON data?
faq.a1: No — it only changes appearance, not content.
faq.q2: Can a formatter fix invalid JSON?
faq.a2: No — but it helps you see where the issue is.
faq.a3: 2 spaces is the most common standard.
faq.q4: Why does my JSON fail to format?
faq.a4: Likely due to syntax errors, missing brackets, or incorrect quote style.

guide.conclusion: Working with raw JSON doesn't have to be frustrating. With a JSON formatter, you can read data instantly, debug faster, and reduce errors — one of the simplest tools that can significantly improve your workflow.
guide.ctaBtn: 👉 Try it here: JSON Formatter Tool
